race.name: Holmenkollmarsjen
race.tagline: Fifty-two kilometers through the Oslo hills to the most famous ski jump in the world — where Norwegian ski culture lives and breathes.

vitals.registration: Online registration, Ski Classics Challengers entry
vitals.prize_purse: Prize money for elite wave via Ski Classics
vitals.aid_stations: Multiple feed stations along the course

climate.description: Oslo's coastal position creates variable winter conditions. Temperatures range from -10°C to 2°C. Maritime influence means snow quality can vary significantly. The elevation gain takes skiers into colder, more reliable snow at higher points on the course.
challenges: Maritime influence creates variable snow conditions
Temperature inversions between valley and hilltop sections
Wax changes needed for elevation-driven temperature shifts

course.primary: Hilly point-to-point through the Nordmarka forest and Oslo hills, finishing at the iconic Holmenkollen ski jump. Over 1,000 meters of climbing makes this one of the most demanding marathon ski courses in Scandinavia.
course.surface: Machine-groomed classic tracks through forest and parkland
course.grooming: High-quality Norwegian standard grooming on established trail network
features: Finishes at Holmenkollen — Norway's national ski shrine
Course through the Nordmarka forest above Oslo
Part of the Ski Classics Challengers series
Technical descents require strong downhill skiing

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Outstanding elevation profile and the Holmenkollen finish make this a demanding, prestigious event. Capital city accessibility is unmatched. Strong Ski Classics Challengers link elevates competitive depth.
nordic_lab_rating.tier_note: Prestige 4 eligible for 1-tier promotion, already at T2

history.summary: Founded in 1974, Holmenkollmarsjen was created to give recreational skiers a mass-start marathon finishing at Norway's most hallowed ski venue — the Holmenkollen ski jump. The course traverses the Nordmarka forest that has been central to Oslo skiing culture for over a century. As part of the Ski Classics Challengers series, it attracts both elite long-distance racers and enthusiastic amateurs tackling one of Scandinavia's most elevation-heavy marathon courses.
notable_facts: Finishes at Holmenkollen, site of the 1952 Olympics and annual World Cup events
Over 1,000 meters of climbing makes it one of the toughest Scandinavian marathons
Part of the Visma Ski Classics Challengers series
The Nordmarka forest has been Oslo's skiing playground since the 1800s
Attracts strong Norwegian domestic field plus international Ski Classics followers
